pkg.purpose: Shared infrastructure and utility leaf layer for the entire monorepo — provides generic HTTP transport (pkg/framework/httptransport), Ent transaction management (pkg/framework/entutils), domain primitives (pkg/models), time/billing utilities (pkg/timeutil, pkg/datetime), Kafka plumbing (pkg/kafka), pagination (pkg/pagination), filters (pkg/filter), and dozens of small focused packages. Primary constraint: pkg/ must never import openmeter/* domain packages — it is a pure leaf dependency used by all seven binaries and their domains.

patterns.name: pkg/framework as universal infrastructure layer
patterns.description: All domain adapter code depends on pkg/framework/entutils for TransactingRepo, pkg/framework/httptransport for the HTTP handler pipeline, pkg/framework/lockr for advisory locks, and pkg/framework/commonhttp for RFC 7807 error encoding. Never bypass these abstractions by calling Ent, net/http, or Postgres APIs directly from domain code.
patterns.example: entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (*Entity, error) { ... })

patterns.name: pkg/models as the foundational domain primitive — zero openmeter/* imports
patterns.description: pkg/models provides ValidationIssue, NamespacedID, ServiceHookRegistry, RFC 7807 Problem, typed error sentinels, and CadencedModel. It must import nothing from openmeter/* — violations create circular deps that break the entire type system.

patterns.name: clock.Now() everywhere in production code
patterns.description: All time reads in production code must use pkg/clock.Now() not time.Now(). Tests use clock.FreezeTime / clock.UnFreeze for deterministic billing period calculations. Forgetting defer clock.UnFreeze() leaks frozen state across test cases.

patterns.name: Typed errors via models.Generic* sentinels
patterns.description: Domain service and adapter methods must return typed errors (models.GenericNotFoundError, models.GenericConflictError, models.GenericValidationError, etc.) not raw fmt.Errorf — the GenericErrorEncoder maps these to correct HTTP status codes via type matching.

patterns.name: pkg/datetime for all calendar arithmetic
patterns.description: Month/year duration arithmetic must use pkg/datetime.DateTime.Add(ISODuration) not time.Duration arithmetic — month lengths vary and naive Duration math produces wrong billing period boundaries.

patterns.name: pkg/filter dual-output filter types
patterns.description: Query filters must implement both Ent selector predicates and go-sqlbuilder WHERE expressions via the pkg/filter.Filter interface. Single-operator validation via Validate() must be called before applying filters to queries.

patterns.name: pkg/pagination Page/Result contract for all list operations
patterns.description: All domain List methods must accept pagination.Page and return pagination.Result[T]. Never compute SQL OFFSET or LIMIT directly; use Page.Offset() and Page.Limit(). Construct results via MapResult/MapResultErr to avoid missed TotalCount assignments.
patterns.example: return pagination.MapResult(page, items, func(i Item) APIItem { return toAPI(i) })

key_file_guides.role: TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue — reads and rebinds the ctx-bound Ent transaction; central to all adapter DB access
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call creator.Tx() directly or store *entdb.Tx on adapter structs — use TransactingRepo so helpers rebind to the caller's ctx transaction

key_file_guides.role: Generic Handler[Request,Response] decode/operate/encode pipeline with chained ErrorEncoders — every domain HTTP handler uses this
key_file_guides.watch_for: Chain adds middleware; AppendOptions appends error encoders — do not replace h.options entirely; GenericErrorEncoder is always appended via defaultHandlerOptions

key_file_guides.role: pg_advisory_xact_lock distributed lock — requires active Postgres transaction in ctx before LockForTX is called
key_file_guides.watch_for: Locker returns an error if no tx is in ctx; use SessionLocker (session.go) for connection-scoped locks that outlive transactions

key_file_guides.role: Immutable ValidationIssue value type with With* copy-on-write builder; carries field paths, component names, severity, and HTTP status attributes
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never mutate a ValidationIssue struct field directly — always use With* methods which clone; direct mutation corrupts shared instances in multi-layer error propagation

key_file_guides.role: Generic ServiceHookRegistry[T] for cross-domain lifecycle callbacks with re-entrancy prevention via pointer-identity context key
key_file_guides.watch_for: Loop-prevention uses fmt.Sprintf('%p', r) — do not copy the registry value; RegisterHooks is called at Wire time in app/common, not in domain constructors

key_file_guides.role: Anchor-based billing period recurrence (Recurrence, NewRecurrence, NewRecurrenceFromISODuration) for subscription and metering cadences
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always construct via NewRecurrence — struct literal skips Validate(); use ISODuration for month/year intervals not time.Duration; Overlaps and OverlapsInclusive differ for touching periods

key_file_guides.role: Typed Kafka config structs producing kafka.ConfigMap with sequential fail-fast validation and localhost IPv4 auto-fix
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never set kafka.ConfigMap keys directly — bypasses validation; metadata.max.age.ms must always be 3× TopicMetadataRefreshInterval

key_file_guides.role: Page/Result[T] contract for all domain List methods; Result.MarshalJSON flattens Page fields into the JSON root
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use MapResult/MapResultErr — manual Result construction misses TotalCount and Page echo; CollectAll caps at 10,000 pages and returns nil on any page error

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/* domain packages from any pkg/ sub-package — creates circular dependencies; pkg/ is a pure leaf that all domains depend on
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in any pkg/ or openmeter/ production code — breaks test determinism for billing period calculations
Returning plain fmt.Errorf from service/adapter code where models.Generic* typed errors are expected — the GenericErrorEncoder falls through to 500 Internal Server Error
Using time.Duration arithmetic for month/year periods instead of pkg/datetime ISODuration — month lengths vary and produce wrong billing period boundaries
Calling entutils.TransactingRepo with a raw *entdb.Client in a helper invoked inside a transaction — must still wrap so the ctx-bound transaction is honored

decisions.decision: pkg/ has zero imports from openmeter/* domain packages
decisions.rationale: Prevents circular dependency; all seven domain binaries and their test suites depend on pkg/ — any openmeter/* import would create an unresolvable cycle

decisions.decision: pkg/framework/entutils.TransactingRepo reads transaction from ctx rather than accepting *entdb.Tx as a parameter
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate implicitly via ctx; parameter-passing would leak tx plumbing into every adapter call site and make nested transaction participation impossible

decisions.decision: pkg/models.ValidationIssue uses a private constructor and With* copy-on-write pattern rather than a mutable builder
decisions.rationale: Immutability prevents accidental mutation as issues propagate through service → adapter → HTTP encoder chain; multiple service layers can annotate the same issue safely

code_examples.scenario: Domain adapter method using TransactingRepo with typed error sentinel

pkg/framework.purpose: Shared low-level infrastructure layer providing the generic HTTP transport pipeline (httptransport), Ent transaction management (entutils), distributed locks (lockr), RFC 7807 error encoding (commonhttp), OTel instrumentation helpers (tracex), and pgx pool wiring (pgdriver). All domain packages under openmeter/ depend on this layer; it must never import them.

patterns.name: httptransport decode/operate/encode pipeline
patterns.description: Every HTTP handler uses httptransport.NewHandler[Request,Response] with a RequestDecoder, an Operation function, and a ResponseEncoder. Error encoders are chained via AppendOptions; the GenericErrorEncoder is always appended last via defaultHandlerOptions.

patterns.name: TxCreator + TxUser[T] triad on every adapter
patterns.description: Every domain adapter must implement Tx() (via HijackTx + NewTxDriver), WithTx() (via NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self(). TransactingRepo reads *TxDriver from ctx and calls WithTx; if none it calls Self(). All three methods are required.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every adapter method body
patterns.description: Every public adapter method body must be wrapped in entutils.TransactingRepo (or TransactingRepoWithNoValue for void returns). This rebinds the Ent client to the active ctx transaction. Helpers called from within a transaction must also wrap — TransactingRepo silently falls back to Self() if no tx is in ctx.

patterns.name: GenericErrorEncoder chain — bool-returning, first-match wins
patterns.description: Error encoders in commonhttp return bool; the first to return true short-circuits the chain. GenericErrorEncoder maps models.Generic* sentinels via HandleErrorIfTypeMatches. Custom encoders passed before it take precedence.
patterns.example: commonhttp.GenericErrorEncoder() // always append last; custom encoders go first

patterns.name: LockForTX inside active Ent transaction
patterns.description: lockr.Locker.LockForTX must be called inside an entutils.TransactingRepo callback where the Ent tx is already in ctx. Calling outside a transaction returns an error. Lock releases on tx commit/rollback automatically.

patterns.name: tracex.Start + Wrap for OTel span lifecycle
patterns.description: Use tracex.Start or tracex.Wrap instead of tracer.Start directly — tracex records errors, sets span status, and recovers panics automatically. Pass the span's ctx to all downstream calls.

patterns.name: Standard mixin composition on all Ent entity schemas
patterns.description: Every new Ent entity schema in openmeter/ent/schema/ must include entutils.IDMixin{}, entutils.NamespaceMixin{}, entutils.TimeMixin{} in its Mixin() slice. Omitting any breaks multi-tenancy queries or soft-delete semantics.

key_file_guides.role: TransactingRepo and TransactingRepoWithNoValue — read *TxDriver from ctx, call WithTx if found or Self() if not; the sole mechanism for ctx-propagated Ent transactions
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never call creator.Tx() directly in adapter method bodies — use TransactingRepo; helpers that take a raw *entdb.Client must still wrap to rebind to ctx tx

key_file_guides.role: IDMixin (ULID char(26) PK), NamespaceMixin (namespace field), TimeMixin (created_at/updated_at/deleted_at), MetadataMixin — standard ent.Mixin implementations
key_file_guides.watch_for: All three standard mixins are required on every new entity; omitting NamespaceMixin makes the entity invisible to namespace-scoped queries

key_file_guides.role: GenericErrorEncoder — chains HandleErrorIfTypeMatches for each models.Generic* type; HandleIssueIfHTTPStatusKnown for ValidationIssue with HTTP status attribute
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always append GenericErrorEncoder last; custom encoders added after it will never fire because bool-returning chain short-circuits on first true

key_file_guides.role: pg_advisory_xact_lock via CRC64 hash of lock key; requires *TxDriver in ctx via entutils.GetDriverFromContext
key_file_guides.watch_for: Returns error if no tx in ctx; do not use context.WithTimeout around LockForTX — use pgdriver.WithLockTimeout instead to avoid pgx connection cancellation

key_file_guides.role: SessionLocker — connection-scoped advisory lock (pg_advisory_lock) for admin flows that need locks to outlive individual transactions
key_file_guides.watch_for: Not goroutine-safe under high contention; always defer sl.Close() to release the dedicated connection; never share across goroutines

key_file_guides.role: Handler[Request,Response] generic struct — ServeHTTP runs decode → operation → encode; Chain adds operation.Middleware; AppendOptions adds error encoders
key_file_guides.watch_for: defaultHandlerOptions always appends GenericErrorEncoder — do not duplicate it; implement decode/encode as pure functions, not methods with side effects

key_file_guides.role: tracex.Start/Wrap wrappers that automatically record errors, set span status, and recover panics — preferred over raw tracer.Start
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use span.Ctx() (not the outer ctx) for downstream calls inside the tracex callback so the span is in the propagated context

key_file_guides.role: pgx pool construction, WithLockTimeout context helper, and pgx TLS configuration for PostgreSQL connections
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use pgdriver.WithLockTimeout instead of context.WithTimeout for advisory lock waits; context cancellation destroys the pgx connection rather than just aborting the wait

anti_patterns: Importing openmeter/* domain packages from any pkg/framework sub-package — creates import cycles; this layer must be a leaf dependency with no domain imports
Using context.Background() or context.TODO() inside adapter TransactingRepo callbacks — always propagate the caller's ctx to maintain transaction and OTel span linkage
Calling creator.Tx() directly or storing *entdb.Tx on adapter structs instead of using TransactingRepo — falls off the ctx-bound transaction and produces partial writes under concurrency
Adding ORDER BY clauses on Ent queries before calling .Cursor() — entcursor appends (created_at ASC, id ASC) and combined ordering becomes undefined
Implementing business logic inside operation.Middleware — middleware is for cross-cutting concerns (auth, logging, tracing) only; business logic belongs in the Operation function

decisions.decision: TransactingRepo reads *TxDriver from ctx rather than accepting *entdb.Tx as a parameter
decisions.rationale: Ent transactions propagate implicitly via ctx; a *entdb.Tx parameter would require threading it through every adapter call site and prevent nested participation via savepoints

decisions.decision: Two advisory lock types: Locker (tx-scoped via pg_advisory_xact_lock) and SessionLocker (connection-scoped via pg_advisory_lock)
decisions.rationale: Tx-scoped locks release on commit/rollback without explicit cleanup; connection-scoped locks are needed for admin operations (e.g., Atlas migrations) that span multiple transactions

decisions.decision: httptransport.Handler[Request,Response] generic pipeline separates decode/operate/encode with chained ErrorEncoders
decisions.rationale: Separation of concerns ensures decode errors, domain errors, and encoding errors each flow through the same encoder chain and produce RFC 7807 Problem Details without boilerplate per-handler

code_examples.scenario: Domain adapter implementing the TxCreator + TxUser triad and using TransactingRepo

pkg/clock.purpose: Process-global test clock that replaces time.Now() with a controllable time source backed by atomic drift/freeze variables. All production code must call clock.Now() so tests can freeze or shift time deterministically without any interface injection.

patterns.name: clock.Now() everywhere in production code
patterns.description: Replace every time.Now() call in production code with clock.Now(). The global atomic design means no interface injection is needed — a single import replaces all time sources.

patterns.name: FreezeTime / UnFreeze in tests with defer
patterns.description: Tests that need deterministic time use clock.FreezeTime(t) to pin the clock and must always defer clock.UnFreeze() or defer clock.ResetTime() to prevent frozen state leaking across test cases.

key_file_guides.role: Package-level atomic drift and freeze vars; Now() reads current time with optional frozen override; SetTime/FreezeTime/UnFreeze/ResetTime control test state
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting defer clock.UnFreeze() or defer clock.ResetTime() leaks state across parallel test cases; Monotonic clock is stripped from all returned times

anti_patterns: Calling time.Now() directly in production code — always use clock.Now()
Forgetting defer clock.ResetTime() or defer clock.UnFreeze() in tests, which leaks frozen/drifted state across test cases
Adding a context parameter or Clock interface — the global atomic design is intentional for zero-overhead usage across the codebase

decisions.decision: Package-level atomics instead of an injected Clock interface
decisions.rationale: Zero-overhead clock reads in hot paths (ingest, balance calculation); no interface injection required across hundreds of call sites

pkg/models.purpose: Foundational domain primitive library shared by all openmeter/* and api/* packages — provides typed error sentinels, immutable ValidationIssue builder, ServiceHookRegistry with re-entrancy prevention, RFC 7807 StatusProblem, FieldDescriptor trees for structured field paths, and time-bounded CadencedModel helpers. Zero imports from openmeter/* domain packages — this package must remain a pure leaf.

patterns.name: Generic typed error sentinels
patterns.description: Service and adapter methods must return models.Generic* typed errors (GenericNotFoundError, GenericConflictError, GenericValidationError, GenericForbiddenError, etc.) so the GenericErrorEncoder chain maps them to correct HTTP status codes via HandleErrorIfTypeMatches.

patterns.name: ValidationIssue immutable with-chain builder
patterns.description: Construct ValidationIssue via NewValidationIssue/NewValidationError/NewValidationWarning then chain With* methods (WithPathString, WithComponent, WithSeverity). Never assign struct fields directly — all With* methods return clones.

patterns.name: ServiceHookRegistry cross-domain lifecycle callbacks
patterns.description: ServiceHookRegistry[T] fans out PreCreate/PostCreate/PreUpdate/PostUpdate/PreDelete/PostDelete to all registered hooks. Loop prevention uses pointer-identity context key — do not copy the registry value. Register hooks in app/common, not in domain constructors.
patterns.example: svc.RegisterHooks(ledgerHook, subjectHook) // called in app/common provider, not constructor

patterns.name: RFC 7807 StatusProblem for HTTP error responses
patterns.description: models.NewStatusProblem reads request-id from Chi context, maps 'context canceled' to 408, suppresses Detail on 500, and serializes to application/problem+json. Use this for all error responses, never write raw JSON error objects.

patterns.name: Validator interface on input types
patterns.description: All service input types (CreateXxxInput, ListXxxInput) must implement models.Validator (Validate() error) so validation runs at service entry before adapter access.

key_file_guides.role: Typed error sentinel constructors: NewGenericNotFoundError, NewGenericConflictError, NewGenericValidationError, etc.; Is* predicate helpers for type matching
key_file_guides.watch_for: Return these typed sentinels from service/adapter code — never plain fmt.Errorf where HTTP status mapping is needed; Is* predicates use errors.As not == comparison

key_file_guides.role: Immutable ValidationIssue value type with private constructor and With* copy-on-write builder; AsValidationIssues traverses error trees
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never mutate a ValidationIssue struct field directly; AsValidationIssues only extracts ValidationIssue nodes — plain errors are invisible to it

key_file_guides.role: Generic ServiceHookRegistry[T] with re-entrancy prevention via pointer-identity context key (fmt.Sprintf('%p', r)); thread-safe fan-out via errors.Join
key_file_guides.watch_for: Register hooks in app/common provider functions — registering inside domain constructors creates circular imports; do not copy the registry value (loop key is pointer-based)

key_file_guides.role: FieldDescriptor tree (via pkg/treex) for structured field paths in validation errors — richer than dot-delimited strings
key_file_guides.watch_for: FieldDescriptor forms a tree not a flat path — use AppendToPath for chaining, not string concatenation

key_file_guides.role: NamespacedID, NamespacedKey, ManagedModel, CadencedModel base structs used throughout domain types
key_file_guides.watch_for: CadencedModel uses half-open [ActiveFrom, ActiveTo) interval — do not use closed intervals for cadence queries

anti_patterns: Importing from openmeter/* domain packages — pkg/models must remain a leaf with zero openmeter/* imports
Mutating a ValidationIssue in place — all With* methods clone; direct struct field assignment bypasses the immutability contract and corrupts shared instances
Returning plain errors.New() from a service layer where ValidationIssue is expected — AsValidationIssues cannot extract raw errors, only ValidationIssue nodes
Using reflect.DeepEqual for Annotations or ValidationIssue equality — use the provided Equal() methods which handle nil and non-comparable values
Constructing StatusProblem with Detail set for 500 responses — NewStatusProblem intentionally suppresses detail on 500 to prevent internal stack trace leak

decisions.decision: ValidationIssue uses a private constructor and copy-on-write With* methods instead of a mutable builder
decisions.rationale: Immutability prevents accidental mutation as issues propagate through service → adapter → HTTP encoder chain; multiple service layers can annotate the same issue independently

decisions.decision: ServiceHookRegistry uses a pointer-identity-based context key (loopKey from fmt.Sprintf('%p', r)) to prevent re-entrant hook invocations
decisions.rationale: Hooks can themselves trigger operations that fire hooks; without loop prevention a billing hook on customer update could trigger infinite re-entrancy

decisions.decision: FieldDescriptor forms a tree (via pkg/treex) rather than a simple dot-delimited string
decisions.rationale: Tree structure allows typed traversal and structured path components with semantic meaning (array index, map key, struct field) rather than opaque string splitting

code_examples.scenario: Service method returning typed error sentinels and ValidationIssue

pkg/pagination.purpose: Offset-based pagination primitives (Page, Result[T], Paginator[T], CollectAll) used by all domain service List methods, plus a cursor-based v2 sub-package for time+ID keyset pagination. Primary constraint: all list queries must use these types — never compute SQL OFFSET or LIMIT directly in handlers or adapters.

patterns.name: Page/Result contract for all list services
patterns.description: All List service methods accept pagination.Page and return pagination.Result[T] and error. Page.Offset() and Page.Limit() provide the SQL OFFSET and LIMIT values. Result[T].MarshalJSON flattens Page fields into the JSON root.
patterns.example: func (s *service) List(ctx context.Context, in ListInput) (pagination.Result[Entity], error)

patterns.name: MapResult/MapResultErr for safe result construction
patterns.description: Use pagination.MapResult or pagination.MapResultErr to transform domain slices into API slices while preserving TotalCount and Page echo. Never construct pagination.Result manually — the Page and TotalCount assignments are easy to get wrong.
patterns.example: return pagination.MapResult(result, func(e Entity) APIEntity { return toAPI(e) }), nil

patterns.name: NewPaginator for CollectAll pagination
patterns.description: Wrap a list function with NewPaginator to get a CollectAll-compatible Paginator[T]. CollectAll caps at MAX_SAFE_ITER = 10,000 pages and returns (nil, error) on any page error — always check the error return.

key_file_guides.role: Page struct with Offset() and Limit() methods; PageNumber and PageSize with defaults
key_file_guides.watch_for: Page.Offset() is (PageNumber-1)*PageSize — page numbers are 1-indexed; passing page 0 produces negative offset

key_file_guides.role: Result[T] generic struct with Items, TotalCount, Page; MarshalJSON flattens Page fields into JSON root
key_file_guides.watch_for: Use MapResult/MapResultErr — manual Result construction misses TotalCount and Page echo; Result.MarshalJSON flattening means Page sub-fields appear at root level in API responses

key_file_guides.role: CollectAll iterates a Paginator[T] up to MAX_SAFE_ITER (10,000) pages; returns nil items on any page error
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always check the error return from CollectAll — it returns nil items on error, so checking only len(items) silently loses data

anti_patterns: Computing SQL OFFSET or LIMIT directly in handlers or service code — always use Page.Offset() and Page.Limit()
Constructing pagination.Result manually instead of using MapResult/MapResultErr — easy to miss TotalCount assignment
Ignoring the error return from CollectAll — returns nil items on error
Implementing a custom Paginator[T] type instead of using NewPaginator
Using cursor-based v2 logic in contexts expecting offset Page/Result — the two contracts are incompatible; choose one per endpoint

decisions.decision: Result[T] flattens Page into the JSON root rather than nesting it under a 'page' key
decisions.rationale: Matches the TypeSpec-generated API response shape where pagination fields appear at root level alongside 'items'

decisions.decision: CollectAll caps at MAX_SAFE_ITER = 10,000 pages and returns (nil, error) rather than partial results on any page error
decisions.rationale: Unbounded collection can exhaust memory; returning nil on error prevents callers from silently processing incomplete data

pkg/filter.purpose: Typed, composable query filters (string, integer, float, time, boolean, ULID) that compile to both Ent selector predicates (func(*sql.Selector)) and go-sqlbuilder WHERE expressions. Every filter type implements the Filter interface and enforces single-operator-per-node validation via Validate().

patterns.name: Dual output — Ent selector and go-sqlbuilder expression
patterns.description: Every filter type implements both Select(predicate) for Ent queries and SelectWhereExpr for go-sqlbuilder ClickHouse queries. Adding a new filter type requires implementing both methods.

patterns.name: Single-operator validation
patterns.description: Each filter node must have at most one operator field set. Validate() returns ErrFilterMultipleOperators if multiple fields are set. Always call Validate() before applying the predicate.
patterns.example: if err := f.Validate(); err != nil { return models.NewGenericValidationError(err) }

patterns.name: IsEmpty / nil guard before applying predicate
patterns.description: A nil or empty Filter returns nil from Select() — applying a nil predicate to an Ent query panics. Always check IsEmpty() or nil before calling Select().

key_file_guides.role: Filter interface definition and all concrete filter types (FilterString, FilterInt, FilterFloat, FilterTime, FilterBool, FilterULID) with And/Or combinators
key_file_guides.watch_for: Contains uses ILIKE with auto-escaped metacharacters — do not pre-escape patterns; Validate() wraps once in models.GenericValidationError at the public boundary

anti_patterns: Setting multiple operator fields on the same filter node — Validate returns ErrFilterMultipleOperators
Applying Select() predicate without nil/IsEmpty check — empty filter returns nil predicate which panics on Ent query
Passing pre-escaped LIKE patterns to $contains/$ncontains — they will be double-escaped
Adding a new filter type without implementing both Select and SelectWhereExpr
Wrapping Validate() result in a second models.GenericValidationError — public Validate() wraps once; internal validateWithComplexity returns raw sentinels

decisions.decision: Dual output (Ent selector + go-sqlbuilder expression) on a single Filter type
decisions.rationale: Billing and metering queries target two different backends (PostgreSQL via Ent, ClickHouse via go-sqlbuilder) — a single filter type eliminates dual maintenance

pkg/timeutil.purpose: Shared time-interval primitives (ClosedPeriod, OpenPeriod, StartBoundedPeriod, Recurrence, Timeline) used throughout billing, subscription, entitlement, and metering to reason about billable windows, recurrence schedules, and event timelines with explicit boundary semantics.

patterns.name: Three Contains variants — pick the correct boundary semantics
patterns.description: Contains uses half-open [from, to) semantics. ContainsInclusive uses [from, to]. ContainsExclusive uses (from, to). For sequential billing periods, use Contains to avoid double-counting at period boundaries.
patterns.example:
// For billing period membership:
if period.Contains(eventTime) { /* charge this event */ }

patterns.name: Recurrence via NewRecurrence — never struct literal
patterns.description: Always construct Recurrence via NewRecurrence or NewRecurrenceFromISODuration which call Validate() to reject zero anchor or non-positive interval. Struct literals bypass validation and silently produce wrong period boundaries.

patterns.name: ISODuration for month/year recurrences
patterns.description: Use datetime.ISODuration (not time.Duration) for month/year recurrences in Recurrence and OpenPeriod — month lengths vary and time.Duration arithmetic produces wrong billing period ends.

patterns.name: OpenPeriod — nil To means unbounded
patterns.description: OpenPeriod uses *time.Time for From and To — nil means the period is open/unbounded in that direction. Touching periods do NOT overlap (Overlaps returns false); use OverlapsInclusive for inclusive-boundary overlap.

key_file_guides.role: Anchor-based Recurrence with iteration via NextAfter/PreviousBefore; Boundary enum controls inclusive/exclusive edge of iteration
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always use NewRecurrence — struct literal skips Validate(); sub-millisecond drift in anchor produces off-by-nanosecond period boundaries

key_file_guides.role: ClosedPeriod and OpenPeriod with Contains, Overlaps, Intersection, Duration helpers
key_file_guides.watch_for: Overlaps and OverlapsInclusive are NOT interchangeable — sequential billing periods must use Overlaps (returns false for touching) to avoid double-counting

key_file_guides.role: Timeline type for ordered sequence of time-bounded entities; used by subscription and entitlement for historical views
key_file_guides.watch_for: Timeline assumes items are ordered by start time — inserting out-of-order produces incorrect ActiveAt results

anti_patterns: Using time.Duration arithmetic instead of ISODuration for month/year recurrences — month lengths vary and Duration math produces wrong billing period ends
Constructing Recurrence{} struct literal without calling NewRecurrence — skips Validate(), allowing zero anchor or non-positive interval
Assuming Overlaps and OverlapsInclusive are interchangeable — sequential billing periods must use Overlaps to avoid double-counting at boundaries
Constructing OpenPeriod{From: &t, To: &t} expecting a valid point interval — Intersection treats same-start-and-end as zero-length and returns nil

decisions.decision: Contains uses half-open [from, to) semantics as the default
decisions.rationale: Half-open intervals compose without overlap for sequential billing periods — adjacent periods [t1,t2) and [t2,t3) never double-count an event at t2

decisions.decision: Recurrence iterates from a floating anchor rather than an epoch
decisions.rationale: Subscription billing periods anchor to the subscription start date, not a global epoch — anchor-based iteration produces correct period starts regardless of when subscription began

pkg/datetime.purpose: Billing-safe datetime and ISO 8601 duration library. DateTime wraps time.Time with RFC 9557 timezone-annotated formatting/parsing; ISODuration wraps rickb777/period with no-overflow month/year arithmetic and DivisibleBy for subscription cadence validation. Primary constraint: all date/calendar arithmetic in billing and subscription must go through this package.

patterns.name: Parse for all datetime inputs
patterns.description: All user-supplied timestamps must go through datetime.Parse (not time.Parse) for RFC 9557 support and timezone annotation preservation.

patterns.name: ISODurationString.Parse() for duration inputs
patterns.description: Duration strings from API inputs must go through ISODurationString.Parse() which validates ISO 8601 format and rejects overflow-prone values.

patterns.name: DateTime.Add(ISODuration) for calendar arithmetic
patterns.description: Use DateTime.Add(ISODuration) for all calendar arithmetic — not time.Time.Add(duration). Month-end clamping is handled by the underlying period library.
patterns.example: next := start.Add(datetime.DurationMonth) // correct month boundary handling

patterns.name: ISODuration.DivisibleBy for cadence validation
patterns.description: Validate that a subscription billing cadence is an exact multiple of a meter aggregation period using ISODuration.DivisibleBy before creating rate cards.

key_file_guides.role: DateTime wrapper around time.Time with RFC9557Layout formatting and Add(ISODuration) calendar arithmetic
key_file_guides.watch_for: DateTime.Format must use RFC9557Layout when the Location has a named timezone — plain RFC3339 loses the IANA zone annotation

key_file_guides.role: ISODuration with no-overflow AddTo, DivisibleBy, and pre-defined DurationXxx constants (DurationDay, DurationWeek, DurationMonth, DurationYear)
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not compare ISODuration with == — use ISODuration.Equal(*ISODuration) which normalises the string form; Constructing period.Period directly bypasses no-overflow arithmetic

anti_patterns: Using time.Time.Add(30 * 24 * time.Hour) for 'one month' — use DateTime.Add(DurationMonth) to handle month-end clamping
Calling time.Parse directly for user-supplied timestamps — always go through datetime.Parse for RFC 9557 support
Comparing ISODuration values with == — use ISODuration.Equal(*ISODuration) which normalises string form
Constructing period.Period directly instead of ISODuration — bypasses no-overflow arithmetic in AddTo

decisions.decision: Custom shiftClockTo for all date arithmetic instead of time.AddDate
decisions.rationale: time.AddDate can overflow month boundaries (Jan 31 + 1 month = Mar 3); shiftClockTo implements correct month-end clamping for billing period boundaries

pkg/kafka.purpose: Shared low-level Kafka infrastructure: typed config structs that compile to kafka.ConfigMap, a TopicProvisioner with LRU cache, librdkafka log bridging to slog, and an OTel metrics sub-package. Primary constraint: pure infrastructure plumbing — no domain or business logic.

patterns.name: ConfigMapper + ConfigValidator interfaces for typed config
patterns.description: Kafka config structs implement ConfigMapper (AsConfigMap() kafka.ConfigMap) and ConfigValidator (Validate() error). Never set kafka.ConfigMap keys directly — always go through AsConfigMap/mergeConfigsToMap which applies the localhost IPv4 auto-fix.
patterns.example:
cfg := kafka.CommonConfigParams{Brokers: "localhost:9092"}
cm, err := kafka.MergeConfigsToMap(cfg, consumerCfg)

patterns.name: TimeDurationMilliSeconds for *.ms keys
patterns.description: Use TimeDurationMilliSeconds wrapper for all Kafka config keys that expect millisecond integer strings — never pass raw time.Duration values.

patterns.name: TopicProvisioner LRU cache
patterns.description: TopicProvisioner caches provisioned topic names in an expirable LRU so duplicate Ingest calls for the same topic skip admin API calls. The cache key is the topic name; expiry is configurable.
patterns.example:
// TopicProvisioner is constructed once in app/common and injected via Wire
// domain code calls Provision(ctx, topicName) — noop if already cached

patterns.name: metadata.max.age.ms always 3× TopicMetadataRefreshInterval
patterns.description: When setting TopicMetadataRefreshInterval, always set metadata.max.age.ms to exactly 3× that value. This prevents premature metadata cache expiry between refreshes.

key_file_guides.role: CommonConfigParams, ProducerConfigParams, ConsumerConfigParams structs with Validate() and AsConfigMap(). configValue interface for string enum validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate is sequential fail-fast — first error wins; localhost IPv4 auto-fix is applied in CommonConfigParams.AsConfigMap not in mergeConfigsToMap

key_file_guides.role: TopicProvisioner wraps the Kafka admin client with an expirable LRU cache keyed by topic name; noop guard prevents re-provisioning
key_file_guides.watch_for: Provisioner is constructed in app/common via KafkaTopicProvisioner; domain code must never call Kafka admin APIs directly

anti_patterns: Setting kafka.ConfigMap keys directly instead of through AsConfigMap/mergeConfigsToMap — bypasses validation and the localhost IPv4 auto-fix
Using Int64Counter or Histogram for librdkafka-derived stats — they are rolling-window point-in-time snapshots; only Int64Gauge is correct
Passing raw time.Duration as a kafka.ConfigMap value for *.ms keys — must use TimeDurationMilliSeconds which renders as millisecond integer string
Setting metadata.max.age.ms independently without tying it to 3×TopicMetadataRefreshInterval
Mixing cooperative and eager PartitionAssignmentStrategy values in ConsumerConfigParams — Validate() explicitly rejects this combination

decisions.decision: ConfigMapper + ConfigValidator split into separate interfaces implemented by value receivers
decisions.rationale: Allows composing multiple config structs via mergeConfigsToMap without requiring a single monolithic config type per binary

decisions.decision: TopicProvisioner uses an expirable LRU cache keyed by topic name
decisions.rationale: High-throughput ingest calls Ingest() per event — hitting Kafka admin API on every call would be prohibitively slow; LRU cache with TTL amortizes admin API overhead
